Basketball In the 2014 NBA playoffs, the San Antonio Spurs beat the Miami Heat. The table below shows the heights of the starters on each team. Use this table to fill in the appropriate symbol $$\text{(=} , \text{unicode[:<]} , \text{unicode[:>])} .$$ \begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Spurs & Height &  & Heat & Height \\ \hline
Tim Duncan & $$\text{83″}$$ &  & Rashard Lewis & $$\text{82″}$$ \\ \hline
Boris Diaw & $$\text{80″}$$ & LeBron James & $$\text{80″}$$ \\ \hline
Kawhi Leonard & $$\text{79″}$$ & Chris Bosh & $$\text{83″}$$ \\ \hline
Tony Parker & $$\text{74″}$$ & Dwyane Wade & $$\text{76″}$$ \\ \hline
Danny Green & $$\text{78″}$$ & Ray Allen & $$\text{77″}$$ \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table} ⓐ Height of Tim Duncan____Height of Rashard Lewis ⓑ Height of Boris Diaw____Height of LeBron James ⓒ Height of Kawhi Leonard____Height of Chris Bosh ⓓ Height of Tony Parker____Height of Dwyane Wade ⓔ Height of Danny Green____Height of Ray Allen

\hline
Mountain & Elevation \\ \hline

Uncompahgre Peak & $$\text{14,309unicode[:']}$$ \\ \hline
Crestone Peak & $$\text{14,294unicode[:']}$$ \\ \hline
Mt. Lincoln & $$\text{14,286unicode[:']}$$ \\ \hline
Grays Peak & $$\text{14,270unicode[:']}$$ \\ \hline
Mt. Antero & $$\text{14,269unicode[:']}$$ \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table} ⓐ Elevation of La Plata Peak____Elevation of Mt. Antero ⓑ Elevation of Blanca Peak____Elevation of Mt. Elbert ⓒ Elevation of Gray’s Peak____Elevation of Mt. Lincoln ⓓ Elevation of Mt. Massive____Elevation of Crestone Peak ⓔ Elevation of Mt. Harvard____Elevation of Uncompahgre Peak
